data.resolution:
4/2/2026 9:35 AM - I departed for client HQ.
4/2/2026 10:40 AM - Arrived at client HQ. I checked in with Tyler.

I went inside and met Gisele. She took me to the server room. Dr. Snook came by and asked for the plan. I let him know I was going to swap the switch and then validate with him and his staff. 

I dismounted the 48 port switch that was not booting correctly from the wall. I adjusted the rack mount on the spare switch and the mounted it. I was about 7 ports shy. The other switch on the wall was full. I tried to pull some items from the switches, but I could not see the back row on either switch well enough to find any unused ports. 

There was a 24 port HP switch in the server rack. I moved the servers to that switch allowing the use of the longer cables to be used to connected the remaining items to the switch on the rack. All three switches are full. 

I checked with the staff and Dr. Snook. They are online. 

I cleaned up the closet and placed the server rack back inside. I checked in with Dr. Snook and then departed. 

4/2/2026 11:46 AM - I updated Tyler and departed for Crown HQ.

4/2/2026 12:24 PM - I arrived and completed my time entry and submitted my mileage. 

I opened a new ticket to evaluate the switch to see if it can be repaired or needs to be replaced.

Building Europe's Leading Cloud Platform

2026 Infrastructure Update

Hello,

At UpCloud, we are continuing to invest in our long-term vision of becoming the European cloud provider of choice. Over the past months, that has included expanding our European footprint with new data centre launches, broadening our AI/GPU capabilities, and continuing to develop more advanced networking and platform features for resilient, sovereign cloud architectures.

As part of that journey, we are updating our Cloud Server lineup 2nd of May 2026.

Our new server lineup

To simplify our offerings, we are introducing two streamlined server tiers available by 2nd of May 2026.

Starter is designed for development, testing, self-hosting, and other cost-conscious workloads. It replaces our current Developer plans. On average 25% less expensive than previous-generation Developer servers.

Premium is designed for production and business-critical workloads where performance and consistency matter most. It will become our default recommendation for production use, combining MaxIOPS storage and modern infrastructure with a clearer more simple positioning in the portfolio.

Plan updates

From 2nd of May 2026, we are streamlining our plans to make it easier to choose the right setup:

-

Developer, General Purpose, High CPU, and High Memory plans will no longer be available for new deployments

-

Existing deployments remain fully supported with no changes to performance, SLAs, or service levels

-

Cloud Native plans remain unchanged

To continue delivering high performance and reliable infrastructure at scale, we are adjusting pricing for a few services. We will introduce pricing adjustments for selected services from 2nd of May 2026: IPv4 addresses, Windows licences and custom images.

This update helps us deliver a clearer Cloud Server lineup while continuing to strengthen the performance, reliability, and capabilities customers expect from UpCloud.

Special note for valued customers

As an important customer, we want to ensure you continue to benefit from our services with predictable costs. Therefore, the generally announced price adjustments for General Purpose server plans will not be applied to your account at this time.

Pricing page
(https://info.upcloud.com/e3t/Ctc/W1+113/cMV5F04/MWr4Yx7B54xW4VKVt15TQhTXW6rg8CD5Mnyn9N61zzTF3l5QzW69sMD-6lZ3k_N7QJfW958YwcW1dYvvV4dSfS9W59Qjs12DnzL3W4p61mn695xD5W4S2vrC3H1RL8W41Dqtf2W9FDBN1bZRTzC0X0-W44Ky3z45Qzc4W1s7xBy5cflypW6jxTrj1HnWlrW6rQGZj35xsZ_W7Qv_vP1Jbt77W4krPh741Ks4mVSk2Vk3c0B9pVrcT1B3YMxNCW4FhvLq5lKpDjW5NrTsj2Vbcv3W5PRNp44k_3wCW77M5D25kXPBNW7TJ6ND7m73NKf6_PW8j04 )

Expanding performance and capacity

We are significantly increasing our investment in specialized compute resources to support your most intensive workloads. All the features will be available in the coming months.

-

Expanded AI / GPU Availability: With new GPU models on the way you’ll soon have even more processing power needed for AI and machine learning.

-

Managed Database Improvement: We are continuously improving our managed database plans, looking to offer even more competitive pricing and high-reliability options.

-

Enhanced Connectivity: Zone to Zone Network Peering will simplify your architecture and improve data transfer efficiency across regions.

If you have any questions about your current services or future deployments, please contact our support team or your UpCloud account representative.

Team UpCloud

Microsoft is working to become carbon negative, water positive, and zero waste by 2030—and that commitment extends to the datacenters that power our cloud. You are invited to join us for Sustainable Cloud Infrastructure in Practice: Microsoft’s Datacenter Strategy for Energy, Water, Carbon, and Materials —an executive-level discussion offering a transparent view into the strategy, priorities, and progress shaping sustainability across our global datacenter portfolio In this session, you’ll hear how Microsoft is translating ambitious goals into operational action—through investments in carbon-free energy and carbon removal, water stewardship initiatives tailored to local conditions, and circular approaches to construction and operations. We’ll also share how these efforts are governed, measured, and disclosed, and what they mean for customers navigating their own sustainability journeys. You’ll leave with a clearer understanding of Microsoft’s approach to sustainable cloud infrastructure and how we’re working to scale impact responsibly and credibly. Why attend? Get a clear, practical view of how Microsoft manages energy, water, carbon, and materials across its global datacenter portfolio. Learn what operational levers matter most—from carbon-free energy and carbon removal to water stewardship and circular construction practices. Understand how these efforts are governed, measured, and disclosed—and how they can support your organization’s sustainability goals.

data.resolution:
Philip let me know that we can internal users to the allow list, but this will bypass any checks and If an internal email is compromised, it will not check for spam, phishing, or malware triggers. 

I gave Everly a call and let her know this 
she was confused because she was marking safe multiple times on different emails but same user 

Since there is noting to be done essentially she said I can close ticket and she will let her team know

data.resolution:
## Summary
-- Investigated SOC alert for failed logon attempts using unknown account "DiagAdmin"; determined activity likely related to invalid/legacy credentials rather than active compromise.

## Detailed Activity, Actions Taken, and Outcome
-- Reviewed RocketCyber SOC alert indicating possible brute force activity targeting domain controller GSFA-CL-DC01
-- Identified source device as internal endpoint GSFA-LPT-13 (192.168.40.177)
-- Analyzed failed logon details:
-- Target Account: DiagAdmin (non-existent account)
-- Logon Type: 3 (network logon, typically SMB/shared resource access)
-- Substatus: 0xc0000064 (username does not exist)
-- Cross-referenced with known accounts and confirmed "DiagUser" is a valid local account, while "DiagAdmin" does not exist
-- Determined behavior consistent with:
-- Cached/stale credentials attempting access to network resources
-- Misconfigured service, mapped drive, or scheduled task using an invalid username
-- No indicators of compromise observed (no lateral movement, no valid account lockouts, no EDR alerts)
-- Conclusion: Likely benign misconfiguration or legacy credential attempt rather than brute force attack
data.title: Client-Alert - Please Investigate Unknown Username DiagAdmin from 192.168.40.177 - GSFA-CL-DC01 - Golden State Fire Apparatus Inc (Managed SOC-PO) - 11034508
